ksa_text: Skill in defining and enforcing enterprise data modeling standards, naming conventions, and structural patterns across engineering teams.

dcwf.top_element_label: Skill in design modeling and building use cases (e.g., unified modeling language).

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA DA-S-003 (Data Architecture/Skill) scores 0.434 composite (highest: DDaT at 0.434). Scope is edge_case for Clinical AI Specialist: 0 in-scope signals, 0 out-of-scope signals. Seniority is appropriate.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency. Include consistent with edge_case scope, Data Architecture, composite 0.434. Decision stands.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence support Data Architecture for Clinical AI Specialist at this seniority?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Data Architecture appears in ~35% of Clinical AI Specialist postings as a secondary competency. Clinical data architecture (FHIR/HL7) relevant for integration-focused roles. Zero role-specific signals insufficient for inclusion.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Revised to exclude. Data Architecture without role signals not market-supported. Final decision: exclude.

decision_rationale: Revised to exclude. Data Architecture without role signals not market-supported. Final decision: exclude.
